title: Vanilla almond nougat
subtitle: Italian-style chewy nougat with whole roasted almonds
excerpt: A classic Italian torrone: a dense, chewy nougat made from honey and sugar syrup beaten into stiff meringue, studded with toasted whole almonds, and set between wafer sheets. Makes about 30 pieces.
sourceNotes: Nougat, a confection of honey, sugar, and egg white beaten together and studded with nuts, is documented throughout Mediterranean confectionery traditions from the medieval period. The Italian form, torrone, is documented from the fifteenth century in Cremona, where it remains a regional speciality. Elizabeth David, Italian Food (1954), includes torrone in the confectionery section, noting its commercial production in Cremona and its resemblance to the French nougat de Montélimar. The form here is the firm, chewy version rather than the soft Viennese nougat.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps wrapped in wax paper in a tin for 2 weeks. Does not tolerate humidity.
recipe.temperatureNote: Stovetop confectionery — no oven required except for toasting almonds

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: Stovetop: sugar syrup cooked to 145°C (hard-crack stage); honey cooked to 130°C separately

glossaryTerms.definition: Nougat is made by streaming hot sugar syrup and hot honey into stiff egg-white meringue with the mixer running. The hot sugar cooks the egg white and progressively stiffens the nougat as it is added. The sugar syrup must be at 145°C and the honey at 130°C — both poured in simultaneously or in sequence, in a steady stream down the side of the bowl. The nougat must be worked into the tin quickly: it stiffens fast as it cools, and once it starts to set it cannot be spread further.

content.text: Two pans on the hob at the same time, both needing constant monitoring, both streaming into a running mixer at different temperatures. This is the most technically demanding recipe in the confectionery section. Read the method fully before starting, have everything ready, and don't attempt it without a stand mixer. The

Toast the {{blanched-almonds}} at 180°C fan for 8–10 minutes until pale gold. Keep warm. Line a 20 × 20 cm tin with baking paper. Put the {{caster-sugar}}, {{glucose-syrup}}, and 60 ml water in one saucepan. Put the {{honey}} in a second saucepan. Attach the {{sugar-thermometer}} to the sugar pan.
Cook the syrups and build the nougat

Begin heating both pans over medium-high heat. Whisk the egg whites from {{eggs}} to stiff peaks in the stand mixer. When the honey reaches 130°C, stream it into the running mixer in a thin, steady flow. When the sugar syrup reaches 145°C, stream it in the same way. Keep whisking on high for 5–8 minutes — the mixture will be very stiff and pulling away from the sides. Beat in the {{vanilla-extract}} and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Fold in the warm {{blanched-almonds}} with a spatula. Work quickly: press into the prepared tin, smooth the surface, and leave to set at room temperature for at least 4 hours. Cut into pieces with an oiled knife.
